cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what the International Olympic Committee (IOC) was concerned about regarding the Beijing 2008 Olympic medals. The options are A to D. First, I need to recall the relevant part of the passage. The last paragraph mentions that experts from the IOC were concerned about the risk of fragility when the jade ring design was proposed. The IOC required the jade rings to be strong enough to withstand a 2-meter drop test, and the Shanghai Mint even raised it to 3 meters. The result was that the medals could survive a 3-meter drop with only minor cracks on the jade rings. So the main concern of the IOC was the fragility of the jade. The question is asking which option matches this concern. Let's look at the options again: A) the medals will be worth less – The passage doesn't mention anything about the monetary value or worth of the medals. It talks about the design and durability, not their value. So probably not A. B) the medals are too difficult to make – The passage does mention that the process is not easy and is a painstaking manual process, but the IOC's concern was specifically about the fragility, not the difficulty of manufacturing. So B might not be correct. C) competitors don't like the design – There's no mention of competitors' opinions about the design. The concern was technical, about the jade's durability.
